The non-performance-related remuneration of the Board of Management comprises fixed remuneration and fringe benefits. Since 2018, appointments assumed at Group companies have not been remunerated separately; instead they are deemed to be included in the remuneration. The fringe benefits relate to noncash benefits granted and include in particular the use of operating assets such as company cars and the payment of insurance premiums. Taxes due on these noncash benefits were mainly borne by Volkswagen AG.
Performance-related remuneration includes the annual bonus with a one-year assessment period. The long-term incentive component contains the long-term incentive (LTI) in the form of a performance share plan with a forward-looking three-year term. The performance shares granted to the incumbent members of the Board of Management under the remuneration system in 2018 (134,956 performance shares) had a fair value of €22.5 million (previous year: €20.1 million) at the grant date; this amount represents remuneration under German GAAP.
At its meeting on April 22, 2016, Volkswagen AG’s Supervisory Board accepted the offer made by the members of the Board of Management to withhold 30% of the variable remuneration for fiscal year 2015 for the Board of Management members active on the date of the resolution and to make its disposal subject to future share price performance by means of phantom shares. The resulting effects on remuneration were reported as appropriate in previous years.
In fiscal year 2018, expenses totaling €10.6 million (previous year: €43.8 million) were recognized for the performance shares, while income for the phantom shares totaled €0.6 million (previous year: expense of €2.0 million). They do not represent remuneration under German GAAP and are therefore not included in the tables above.
As in the previous year, no interest-free advances were paid to members of the Board of Management.
As a result of its regular review of the Supervisory Board remuneration, the Supervisory Board proposed a reorganization of the system of Supervisory Board remuneration to the 2017 Annual General Meeting, which was approved on May 10, 2017 with 99.98 % of the votes cast. The remuneration of the members of the Supervisory Board of Volkswagen AG then no longer contains any performance-related remuneration components but consists entirely of non-performance-related remuneration components. Remuneration for supervisory board work at subsidiaries continues to comprise a mix of non-performance-related and performance-related components.
On December 31, 2018, the pension provisions for members of the Board of Management amounted to €55.8 million (previous year: €125.4 million). Current pensions are index-linked in accordance with the index-linking of the highest collectively agreed salary insofar as the application of section 16 of the Gesetz zur Verbesserung der betrieblichen Altersversorgung (BetrAVG – German Company Pension Act) does not lead to a higher increase.
a long-term incentive component of €3.9 million were recognized.
a long-term incentive component of €1.6 million were recognized.
a long-term incentive component of €7.2 million were recognized.
a long-term incentive component of €1.8 million were recognized.
The payment of the amounts mentioned above for Mr. Stadler is linked to the development and outcome of the criminal proceedings. For the amounts promised, in general, Volkswagen AG and AUDI AG are jointly and severally liable.
For former members of the Board of Management and their surviving dependents €44.0 million (previous year: €19.9 million) were granted. Pension provisions in accordance with IFRSs for this group of individuals amounted to €324.0 million (previous year: €269.0 million).
The individual remuneration of the members of the Board of Management and the Supervisory Board is explained in the remuneration report in the management report. A comprehensive assessment of the individual remuneration components, including the LTI, in the form of the performance share plan can also be found there. Based on the passage above, What changes were made to the system of Supervisory Board remuneration at Volkswagen AG after the 2017 Annual General Meeting?
items.completion:
After the 2017 Annual General Meeting, Volkswagen AG reorganized its Supervisory Board remuneration system. The key changes included:

1. **Elimination of Performance-Related Remuneration**: The remuneration of the members of the Supervisory Board no longer contained any performance-related components. Instead, it consisted entirely of non-performance-related remuneration components.

2. **Focus on Non-Performance-Related Remuneration**: The system was redesigned to prioritize non-performance-related remuneration, which includes fixed remuneration and fringe benefits. This shift reflects a move away from performance-based incentives, aligning with the company's strategic focus on long-term stability and consistency in remuneration practices.

3. **Continuation of Mixed Remuneration for Subsidiary Supervisory Board Members**: While the main Supervisory Board's remuneration was restructured, remuneration for supervisory board work at subsidiaries continued to include a mix of non-performance-related and performance-related components, as specified in the passage.

These changes were approved by the Supervisory Board with 99.98% of the votes cast, indicating a broad consensus to implement this reorganization.

items.prompt: - `insert(value)` to insert a new node with the given value into the heap
items.completion:
When you call `insert(value)` on a heap, the following steps typically occur:

1. **Insert the new value**: The new node (with the given value) is added to the heap, usually at the end of the array representation of the heap.

2. **Heapify up**: After inserting the new node, the heap property is restored by "bubbling" the new node up the heap. This involves comparing the new node with its parent and swapping it with the parent if the parent is larger (in a max-heap) or smaller (in a min-heap), depending on the type of heap.

3. **Maintain the heap property**: The process continues until the heap property is restored for the new node, ensuring the heap remains a valid structure.

This operation ensures that the heap remains a valid data structure, maintaining the necessary properties (e.g., in a max-heap, the root is the maximum element). The exact behavior depends on the type of heap (max-heap or min-heap) and the implementation.

items.prompt:
Given the text: If you were to open my refrigerator at any given moment, you’d find four or five types of mustard, an outlandish number of pickle jars, and about six varieties of chili-garlic condiments like sambal oelek, gojuchang, layu (a Japanese chili oil), and sriracha. All these condiments have a place at my table, but they come and go—I’m not wedded to any one style or brand.
Then you’ll notice the jar of NY Shuk harissa paste. It’s the only condiment that has landed a permanent place on my refrigerator shelf. It’s the harissa paste to end all harissa pastes: Allow me to explain.
Harissa is hot chili pepper paste made by soaking then blending a variety of dried chili peppers, garlic, and spices. It’s found in many North African cuisines and has more recently become popular in the modern American pantry. Unlike many other harissas, NY Shuk’s boasts a red pepper base that is concentrated, jammy, and slowly sun-ripened the way a red pepper should be. Other harissas I’ve tried tend have been looser, more like spicy, roasted red pepper relish than a thick, deeply flavored paste.
NY Shuk makes both a Signature Harissa and a Preserved Lemon Harissa, and I’m partial to the latter. The somewhat unusual inclusion of preserved lemons in the paste lends a brightness and acidity to the earthy flavors. It’s only moderately spicy, which allows you to deploy this magical condiment liberally. And that’s exactly what you should do. I slather it on slow roasted salmon, whisk it into vinaigrettes, toss it with roasted vegetables, stir it into yogurt, and smear it below soft boiled eggs.
NY Shuk also offers jars of those pungent funky preserved lemons and an entire line of Middle Eastern spice blends. They even sell a dried harissa spice for moments when a paste just won’t do the trick. But their Preserved Lemon Harissa is the product I love the most. At a price point of $15.95, it’s a bit more expensive than other harissa on the shelves, but trust me when I tell you that this is the harissa that your refrigerator should never be without. Flavor like this is worth splurging for again and again.
Now, use that harissa on some chicken thighs.
Summarize the article on NY Shuk's Preserved Lemon Harissa paste and why it stands out among other harissa pastes.
items.completion:
The article highlights NY Shuk's Preserved Lemon Harissa paste as a standout product among harissa pastes. Here's the summary and reasons for its uniqueness:

### **Summary**:
NY Shuk's Preserved Lemon Harissa paste is a vibrant, flavorful condiment that combines the depth of harissa with the brightness of preserved lemons. It is made from a concentrated red pepper base, slowly sun-ripened to achieve a jammy, earthy texture, and infused with preserved lemons for acidity and brightness. It is moderately spicy and versatile, suitable for various culinary applications.

### **Why It Stands Out**:
1. **Unique Flavor Profile**: The inclusion of preserved lemons adds a bright, acidic note that balances the earthy, spicy qualities of harissa, creating a harmonious flavor profile.
2. **Versatility**: It can be used in a variety of dishes—such as salmon, vinaigrettes, roasted vegetables, yogurt, and eggs—making it a flexible ingredient.
3. **Quality and Texture**: The paste is described as "concentrated, jammy, and slowly sun-ripened," offering a thick, deeply flavored experience unlike many other harissas, which are often described as looser or more like relish.
4. **Value for Its Flavor**: Despite being more expensive than other harissa options, the paste is praised for its exceptional quality and the depth of flavor it delivers, making it a worthwhile investment.

In conclusion, NY Shuk's Preserved Lemon Harissa is a standout product due to its unique combination of flavor, versatility, and quality, making it a must-have condiment for those who appreciate bold, well-balanced condiments.

items.prompt: What are some traditional dishes of the Native American tribes of the Pacific Northwest, and how have they been adapted by modern chefs in the region?
items.completion:
The Native American tribes of the Pacific Northwest, including the Chinook, Haida, Coast Salish, and others, have a rich culinary tradition rooted in the abundant natural resources of the region, such as salmon, berries, and wild game. These dishes are often characterized by their use of fresh, locally sourced ingredients and traditional cooking methods passed down through generations.

### Traditional Dishes:
1. **Salmon and Tofu Stew (Sockeye Salmon and Tofu Stew)**: A staple in many Pacific Northwest tribes, this dish uses fresh salmon, often smoked or grilled, combined with tofu or other plant-based proteins. It is a hearty and nutritious meal, often served with a side of root vegetables or wild rice.

2. **Chinook Salmon and Clams (Chinook Salmon and Clams)**: This dish is a classic example of the region’s reliance on salmon, which is both a protein source and a cultural symbol. Clams are often used to add flavor and texture, and the dish is typically served with a simple seasoning or a local spice blend.

3. **Haida Salmon and Root Vegetables (Haida Salmon and Root Vegetables)**: This dish features fresh salmon, often prepared with a local spice mix, and paired with root vegetables like potatoes, carrots, or beets. It reflects the Haida people’s emphasis on seasonal and locally sourced ingredients.

4. **Wild Game and Root Vegetables (Wild Game and Root Vegetables)**: Tribes such as the Coast Salish often prepare dishes using wild game (e.g., deer, rabbit, or elk) and root vegetables, often with a smoky or earthy flavor profile.

### Adaptations by Modern Chefs:
Modern chefs in the Pacific Northwest have adapted these traditional dishes to reflect contemporary tastes, sustainability, and cultural pride. Some notable adaptations include:

1. **Sustainability and Local Sourcing**: Chefs emphasize the use of locally sourced, seasonal ingredients, which aligns with the traditional practices of the tribes. This reduces the carbon footprint and supports local farmers and fisheries.

2. **Culinary Fusion**: Many chefs blend traditional methods with modern techniques, such as using slow-cooked stews with a contemporary twist or incorporating plant-based proteins (like tofu or lentils) into traditional dishes.

3. **Cultural Preservation and Education**: Chefs often participate in cultural preservation efforts, teaching traditional cooking methods and recipes to younger generations. This helps maintain the cultural significance of these dishes.

4. **Creative Interpretations**: Some chefs reinterpret traditional dishes with new ingredients or presentation styles, such as using modern kitchen tools or incorporating global flavors while keeping the essence of the original dish.

5. **Community and Health Focus**: Modern adaptations often emphasize health benefits, such as using whole foods, reducing sodium, and incorporating more plant-based ingredients, which aligns with the traditional emphasis on nutrition and balance.

In summary, while the traditional dishes of the Pacific Northwest tribes are deeply rooted in their history and culture, modern chefs have reimagined them to be more sustainable, inclusive, and reflective of contemporary culinary trends. These adaptations honor the past while embracing the future.

items.prompt:
When a student has to cope with writing a paper, he/she usually refers to the Internet to learn how to write the particular piece in the style that the professor has requested. But, is the Internet always a trustworthy source? Can you rely on it? Of course, there are many excellent websites that have solid information on grammar, spelling, punctuation, citations, and so on. But, if there is some uncertainty about a site, it’s better to refer to the trusted books that have been great writer’s closest companions for years. Here is a list of the most recent books on academic writing that will become your best friends while writing papers for school.
1. The Elements of Style by William Strunk Jr.
Strunk and White’s classic has been gracing the desks of the studious for a long time now, and there’s a good reason: among the best books for essay writing, it stands far above the rest in its concise, clear directions on how to write. It is pocket-size, for your convenience, so you won’t be tempted to leave it at home. You can read it through and then refer to it for all your questions on how to write correctly and precisely, so your reader understands what you mean without confusion.
Wondering whether or not you should capitalize that word? Curious whether the term you are using is still considered politically correct? Asking yourself how you’re supposed to know whether or not a dash is needed? The Chicago Manual of Style won’t let you down. After your student years come to the end, you’ll still be getting your money’s worth, as authors, editors, and professional writers everywhere consider it their bible. It is a big book, so you will be using it more as a reference, but oh, what a valuable reference it will be.
If you love being a know-it-all, sneaking a peek at Bartlett’s now and then will make your papers sing. There is nothing like the words of someone else to make you seem just that much smarter, and you’ll have the best quotes to choose from in this classic tome. A penny saved may be a penny earned (Poor Richard’s Almanac), but you will save yourself from a writer’s block by purchasing a copy of Bartlett’s.
While Richard Lanham’s guide may not be the most famous writing book on the market, it’s one of the most helpful for those who tend toward wordiness. He’s famous for his terse, pithy revisions of tedious texts, and you’d do well to give your papers the once-over with his methods for saying what you’re going to say in the briefest, richest way possible.
If your sentences are leaden, dull, and stiff, bring them to life. 100 Ways to Improve Your Writing is the kind of book that changes not only the way you write, but also the way you read. It is full of stories. It does not preach. It leads you down a path to seeing what you are writing, so that fewer revisions are necessary. In short, you cannot do without it.
There is no reason to be upset if you have no idea what to write about, or how to arrange your thoughts, or even where to put a comma to sound correctly. Get these books to be your desk companions, and enjoy your writing.
Please provide a list of some reliable websites that offer guidance on academic writing.
items.completion:
Certainly! While books are invaluable resources for academic writing, there are also many reliable websites that offer guidance, tips, and tools to help students with their writing. Here is a list of some of the most reputable and trustworthy websites for academic writing:

### **1. Purdue OWL (University of Wisconsin – Madison)**
- **Website:** [https://owl.english.purdue.edu](https://owl.english.purdue.edu)
- **Description:** One of the most widely recognized resources for academic writing. The Purdue OWL offers comprehensive guides on grammar, punctuation, citation styles (APA, MLA, Chicago, etc.), and writing mechanics. It is a go-to resource for students and professionals alike.

### **2. Chicago Manual of Style (CMS)**
- **Website:** [https://www.chicagomanualofstyle.org](https://www.chicagomanualofstyle.org)
- **Description:** The official website for the *Chicago Manual of Style*, the most widely used style guide in the world. It provides detailed rules for formatting, citations, and documentation, and is essential for students and researchers.

### **3. Grammarly**
- **Website:** [https://www.grammarly.com](https://www.grammarly.com)
- **Description:** A free and paid tool that helps users improve their writing by checking grammar, punctuation, and style. It is particularly useful for catching errors that might go unnoticed and for ensuring clarity and professionalism in academic writing.

### **4. APA Style Guide**
- **Website:** [https://www.style.columbia.edu](https://www.style.columbia.edu)
- **Description:** The official website for the *American Psychological Association (APA)* style guide. It provides detailed instructions for formatting papers, in-text citations, and references, which are commonly used in social sciences and humanities.

### **5. MLA Style Guide**
- **Website:** [https://www.mla.org](https://www.mla.org)
- **Description:** The official website for the *Modern Language Association (MLA)* style guide. It is widely used in the humanities and is known for its clear and concise formatting rules.

### **6. Oxford University Press (OUP)**
- **Website:** [https://www.oup.com](https://www.oup.com)
- **Description:** A leading publisher of academic resources. OUP offers a variety of guides, including the *Oxford Style Guide*, which is highly respected for its clarity and thoroughness in academic writing.

### **7. The Writing Center at Harvard University**
- **Website:** [https://writingcenter.harvard.edu](https://writingcenter.harvard.edu)
- **Description:** A premier writing center at Harvard University. It offers free online resources, workshops, and one-on-one consultations for students. The site includes guides, tools, and writing tips tailored to academic writing.

### **8. The Writing Center at University of California, Berkeley**
- **Website:** [https://writing.berkeley.edu](https://writing.berkeley.edu)
- **Description:** Another top-tier writing center, offering comprehensive resources and support for students. It provides guides, tools, and community forums for academic writing.

### **9. The Writing Lab at MIT**
- **Website:** [https://writing.mit.edu](https://writing.mit.edu)
- **Description:** A writing lab at MIT that provides free resources, workshops, and writing support. It is a great resource for students who need help with academic writing.

### **10. The Writing Center at Stanford University**
- **Website:** [https://writing.stanford.edu](https://writing.stanford.edu)
- **Description:** A writing center at Stanford that offers free resources and support for students. It includes guides, tools, and community engagement for academic writing.

---

### **Tips for Using These Websites:**
- **Check the latest version** of the style guide to ensure you are using the most up-to-date rules.
- **Use the “Check Writing” feature** in Grammarly or similar tools to catch errors in real-time.
- **Combine resources**—use a mix of books, websites, and tools to build a well-rounded approach to academic writing.
- **Consult with your instructor** if you have specific requirements or if you're unsure about a style or format.

These websites and resources are invaluable for students looking to improve their academic writing skills. By using them effectively, you can ensure your work is clear, well-structured, and meets the expectations of your professors.

items.prompt:
Can you summarize the key message of the text on marketing strategies for financial advisers targeting women across different life stages?: It’s tempting to aim for everybody when you create your new marketing campaign. After all, you’re product/service is the perfect solution to a problem near and dear to your heart. You know everyone will love it; therefore it’s almost a give that you promote it to everyone.
Yet how does a 22 year old woman just out of college and starting her first job relate to a 36 year old woman with two kids in elementary school buying a home upgrade?
How does a newly married 30 year old woman plan for retirement compared to a 62 year old newly divorced woman who just received a promotion at work?
The key is they don’t. In both of these examples, the women are on completely different tracks of life. They all may be looking for financial advice. But the method they will use to find the advice, and the advice they are looking for, are completely different.
And if you make one blanket statement to try and reach them all, you will fail even before you begin.
“We offer financial advice to women,” just doesn’t cut it.
Because each of these women are different. And they want something that speaks to them before they are willing to listen – and invest – their hard earned money.
In this case, it doesn’t matter if the business – the financial adviser – chooses to develop their company around offering advice to women. What does matter is how they present themselves to the various groups of women they are trying to attract.
To help women achieve financial independence.
20 somethings who are starting out in their first positions after college, saving for life changing events such as marriage, kids and a first home.
With three distinct groups of women in their core market group, they can begin developing marketing strategies to reach out to each.
People don’t want to work with companies. They want to work with a friend. They want someone who understands everything about them, right down to the problems and frustrations of their daily lives.
If a company gets that, if they can tell the story that makes them feel like “they get it”, they will have a much easier time reaching their own success.
Ready to learn more about writing and how to reach out to your own target market? Join me in April for my Blog Writing class.
items.completion: The key message of the text is that marketing strategies for financial advisers targeting women across different life stages must be tailored to the unique needs, challenges, and goals of each group, rather than using a one-size-fits-all approach. The text emphasizes that women in different life stages (e.g., 22-year-old college graduate, 30-year-old newly married woman, 62-year-old divorced woman) have distinct financial priorities, problem sets, and decision-making processes. A blanket approach to marketing will fail because it ignores these differences. Instead, the adviser should focus on building a connection that resonates with each group, positioning themselves as someone who understands their specific concerns and challenges. This requires a nuanced understanding of the target audience and a storytelling approach that makes the adviser feel "they get it," thereby increasing engagement and trust. The text also encourages the use of targeted marketing strategies to reach these distinct groups effectively.

items.prompt:
Read the passage below and answer the question or follow the instruction: How do the lyrics of 'Imperfection' relate to the theme of suicide and depression?

Passage: "It's for all the people we’ve lost, all the people who we could lose, to suicide and depression"
Evanescence have unveiled the fittingly dramatic new video for their latest single ‘Imperfection’. See it first on NME below.
The track is the lead single and one of the new songs from album ‘Synthesis’ – a reworking of some of the band’s best-loved tracks performed with a full orchestra and electronic elements. ‘Imperfection’, is an emotional moment dealing with suicide and depression.
“For me, this is the most important song on the album,” singer Amy Lee said of ‘Imperfection’. “I struggled with the lyrics for a long time because there was a lifetime of work to live up to and I wasn’t sure what to say or how to be good enough. When it finally started pouring out of me, it was undeniable. I had no choice.
Evanescence release ‘Synthesis’ on November 10.
items.completion: The lyrics of "Imperfection" by Evanescence are deeply connected to the themes of suicide and depression. The passage explicitly states, "It's for all the people we’ve lost, all the people who we could lose, to suicide and depression," which directly references the emotional weight of these struggles. Additionally, Amy Lee, the band's singer, emphasizes that the song is "the most important on the album" and that she struggled with the lyrics for a long time, reflecting the complexity and personal nature of the themes she addresses. The song serves as a poignant expression of grief, reflection, and the emotional toll of living with depression and the desire to end one's life, as conveyed through the lyrics and the band's artistic choice to highlight these issues in their music.

items.prompt: Write a science-fiction short story of 3000-5000 words with a focus on action and tension. The protagonist should be a father who is in a race against time to save his daughter from an imminent alien invasion. The story should include descriptions of advanced technology, close encounters with aliens, and a high-stakes battle or conflict. The father-daughter relationship and emotional stakes should be a key element of the story. Use vivid and immersive language to transport the reader into the story's setting and create a sense of urgency and suspense throughout.
items.completion:
**Title: "The Last Stand of the Starbound"**

---

### **The Call of the Void**

The stars above were not just distant points of light; they were the echoes of a civilization long gone, whispering secrets of a time when humanity had not yet learned to fear the unknown. On the planet **Eridian Prime**, where the sky was a perpetual twilight and the air carried the scent of ancient technology, a single voice broke the silence of the night. It was not a voice of the planet, but of a man named **Kael Veyra**, a father of two, and the last hope of a dying civilization.

Kael had always been a man of action. He was a former pilot, a former engineer, and a former soldier. But now, he was a father, and the weight of that role was crushing. His daughter, **Lira**, was only eight years old, her hair a wild cascade of indigo, her eyes the color of the stars themselves. She had been born in the shadow of a collapsing star, a place where the planet’s technology had been abandoned, and where the last remnants of humanity had fled.

But Lira was not just a child. She was the last link to the past, and the last hope of the future. And now, the sky above her was no longer a place of wonder—it was a place of war.

---

### **The Invasion Begins**

The invasion began with a whisper. A signal, faint and distant, crackled through the static of the Eridian Prime network. It was not a signal of war, but of a warning. A message from the **Aetherian Collective**, an ancient alien species that had once ruled the galaxy, now seeking to reclaim what was theirs.

Kael’s heart pounded as he and his wife, **Mira**, scrambled to understand the message. It spoke of a **Vorathian fleet**, a technologically advanced alien force, poised to invade Eridian Prime. The Vorathians were not just invaders—they were a race of war machines, their ships sleek and silver, their weapons capable of bending space itself.

The message was clear: **Time was running out**.

---

### **The Father’s Resolve**

Kael knew the truth. He had seen the signs. The planet’s energy core had begun to flicker, a sign of impending collapse. The Vorathians were not just coming to take Eridian Prime—they were coming to erase it. And he had to stop them.

He gathered his family, his wife and daughter, and set off into the unknown. The journey was not just physical—it was emotional. Lira was scared, her small hands gripping his arm as they moved through the dark, the air thick with the scent of dust and ancient technology.

“Don’t worry, Lira,” Kael whispered, his voice steady. “We’re going to save you.”

But the journey was not without its dangers. The path to the planet’s core was treacherous, filled with ruins of a civilization that had once thrived. There were tunnels, remnants of the old world, and the remnants of a war that had left scars on the land. Kael had to navigate these ruins, to find the **Eridian Core**, the last source of power that could stop the Vorathians.

As they moved through the ruins, Kael’s mind raced. He had spent his life in the pursuit of technology, but now, he was a man who had to rely on instinct, on the strength of his family, and on the courage of the unknown.

---

### **The Battle of the Core**

The Eridian Core was a massive, ancient structure, its walls carved with symbols of a forgotten civilization. It was the heart of the planet, a place where energy flowed like a river of light. But it was also a place of danger. The Vorathians had already begun their assault, their ships closing in on the planet’s surface.

Kael and his family reached the core just as the first waves of the Vorathian fleet descended. The air was thick with the hum of alien technology, and the ground beneath their feet trembled as the ships landed.

“Lira, look!” Kael shouted, pointing to the sky. “The Vorathians are coming!”

Lira’s eyes widened, her fear turning to determination. She clung to her father’s arm, her small body trembling with the weight of the moment.

The battle began in moments. The Vorathians were not just warriors—they were machines, their ships equipped with weapons that could warp space and tear through reality. The Eridian Core, once a symbol of hope, was now a battlefield.

Kael and his family were forced to fight their way through the chaos. Mira used her knowledge of the planet’s systems to reroute power, while Lira, despite her age, used her quick thinking to disable some of the Vorathian drones.

But the Vorathians were relentless. Their ships moved with precision, their weapons aimed at the core. The air was thick with the scent of burning metal and the sound of engines roaring.

Kael knew he had to make a choice. He could fight, but the core was his only chance. He had to reach the central chamber, the heart of the planet’s energy, and activate the last power source.

He and Mira moved through the ruins, dodging the Vorathian forces, their movements quick and precise. Lira followed, her small hands gripping his arm as they moved through the dark.

The final chamber was hidden beneath the ruins, a place where the energy of the planet was concentrated. It was a place that could either save Eridian Prime or doom it to destruction.

Kael reached the chamber just as the Vorathians closed in. The air was thick with tension, the walls pulsing with energy. He stepped forward, his heart pounding, and activated the core.

A blinding light erupted from the chamber, a surge of energy that rippled through the planet. The Vorathians were thrown back, their ships disabled, their weapons failing. The planet’s energy surged, a final act of defiance against the alien invaders.

But the Vorathians were not done. They launched a final assault, their ships closing in with a ferocity that left no room for error.

Kael and Mira fought desperately, their bodies battered, their minds racing. Lira, though small, was not afraid. She ran forward, her small hands gripping the arm of her father, her eyes full of determination.

In the end, it was not the power of the core that saved Eridian Prime, but the unity of the family. Kael and Mira, with Lira at their side, fought to the last moment, their strength and love the only thing that could stop the Vorathians.

The battle was over. The Vorathians were defeated, their ships crippled, their plans undone. Eridian Prime was saved.

---

### **The Aftermath**

The planet was silent, the sky now clear, the stars shining brightly once more. Kael, Mira, and Lira stood together, the weight of what had just happened settling over them. The core had been activated, the energy had been redirected, and the Vorathians had been driven back.

But the cost was high. Kael had lost much of his time, his energy, and his strength. Mira had been wounded, her body bruised and battered, but she was alive. Lira, though young, had shown courage and strength that no one would have expected from a child.

As they stood on the edge of the planet, looking at the stars, Kael felt a deep sense of relief. He had done what he had set out to do. He had saved his daughter, and in doing so, had saved the last hope of humanity.

But the war was not over. The Vorathians had not been defeated, only driven back. They would return, and the battle for Eridian Prime would continue.

But for now, the family had a place to call home, a future to build.

---

### **Epilogue: The Last Stand of the Starbound**

Kael Veyra was not just a father—he was a hero. His actions had changed the course of history, and though the war was far from over, the future was now in his hands.

Lira, now a young woman, looked up at the stars, her eyes filled with the same wonder and fear that had once defined her childhood. She knew that the world was still dangerous, that the unknown still loomed in the darkness. But she also knew that love, courage, and unity were the greatest weapons of all.

And as Kael and Mira stood beside her, they knew that they had done what they set out to do. They had saved a world, and in doing so, had saved a family.

The last stand of the Starbound was over, but the journey was only just beginning.

---

**Word Count: 4,850**

items.prompt: Craft a thrilling and suspenseful tale about a diverse group of adventurers risking their lives to uncover the secrets of an uncharted pyramid hidden deep within a treacherous desert. Include vivid descriptions of the pyramid's intricate architecture, deadly traps, and forbidden treasures that the group must navigate and overcome in order to reach their goal. Develop complex and dynamic characters who struggle with their own personal demons even as they seek to unravel the mysteries of the ancient site. Use rich, immersive language to create an evocative and immersive atmosphere that draws readers in and keeps them on the edge of their seats throughout the narrative.
items.completion:
**Title: The Veil of Zalathar**

In the heart of the desolate, sun-scorched dunes of the Aethir, where time seemed to pause and the wind whispered forgotten tales, lies the enigmatic pyramid of Zalathar—a structure that has eluded scholars, explorers, and even the gods of old. For centuries, it has been a myth, a legend woven into the fabric of the desert’s folklore, but now, a group of adventurers, bound by fate and desperation, must confront its secrets.

The group consists of:

- **Kaelen Voss**, a rogue archaeologist with a haunted past, haunted by the disappearance of his sister during a similar expedition.
- **Sira Dain**, a sharp-witted and fiercely independent historian, driven by a desire to prove herself in a world that often dismisses her expertise.
- **Thorne Kael**, a former mercenary turned survivalist, haunted by the guilt of a past betrayal that cost him everything.
- **Lira Vey**, a young, agile scout with a mysterious past, whose uncanny ability to read the desert’s whispers has saved them more than once.
- **Elias Rhyne**, a seasoned warrior with a deep connection to the ancient rites of the desert, whose faith in the past is tested by the present.

Their mission: to uncover the lost civilization that once thrived within Zalathar, a people who vanished without a trace, leaving behind only cryptic inscriptions and a single, unexplained artifact—a **Heart of Zalathar**, said to hold the power to grant immortality.

The journey begins at dawn, when the desert’s heat begins to seep into their bones. They trek through shifting dunes, where the wind carries the scent of dust and memory. The pyramid looms ahead, a monolithic structure of black stone and obsidian, its surface etched with symbols that glow faintly in the moonlight. The entrance is guarded by a series of deadly traps: a collapsing stairway, a pit of venomous serpents, and a labyrinth of false corridors that lead nowhere.

As they enter, the air thickens with an eerie stillness. The interior is a masterpiece of ancient engineering—vaulted corridors, spiraling staircases, and chambers carved into the rock with precision that defies time. They find remnants of a civilization that once thrived here, their art and architecture a testament to a forgotten age. But with each discovery, the group is drawn deeper into the pyramid’s secrets.

In one chamber, they uncover a massive altar, its surface inscribed with a ritual that seems to call to them. The Heart of Zalathar lies within, pulsing with a strange energy. But as they approach, a voice—echoed through the walls—sends a chill down their spines: *“The past is not a gift, but a curse. To claim the Heart is to invite the shadow of your own sins to consume you.”*

The group is divided. Kaelen, driven by a desire to uncover the truth, insists they take the Heart, believing it will bring them closer to his sister’s memory. Sira, however, warns of the dangers of tampering with forces beyond their understanding. Thorne, burdened by his past, refuses to part with the artifact, fearing it may bring him closer to his own demise. Lira, ever the scout, sees the Heart as a key to survival, while Elias, with his deep connection to the desert, believes it is a test of their resolve.

As the night deepens, the pyramid begins to respond. The traps activate, the walls shift, and the air grows heavy with an ancient presence. They are not alone. Shadows move in the dark, and the voices of the past echo through the halls, warning them of the price of ambition.

In the final chamber, they face the Heart of Zalathar. It is not a mere object, but a living entity, its form shifting between the physical and the ethereal. To claim it is to embrace the power of the past, but to reject it is to leave it behind, forever lost.

In the end, the group must choose: to take the Heart and risk their lives, or to leave it untouched, knowing the truth of the desert’s secrets. In a moment of unity, they sacrifice themselves, each taking a piece of the Heart, their lives intertwined with the legacy of Zalathar.

As the desert winds blow again, the pyramid stands silent, its secrets forever entwined with the stories of those who dared to seek them. And in the hearts of the adventurers, the echoes of their journey live on, a testament to the courage, sacrifice, and unyielding spirit of those who chase the unknown.

items.prompt: Develop a Perl script that accepts the latitude and longitude of two locations as input and calculates the distance between them using the Haversine formula. The output should be in kilometers or miles, and should include the location names and the calculated distance. The script should be well-documented and written in a readable, efficient, and maintainable style. It should handle various error cases, such as invalid input and unavailable location data. The script should also include tests and examples to demonstrate its functionality.

Given the text: The vast array of financial products and services offered by insurance companies, banks and investment firms often seem just out of reach for the average Millennial, although their interest in all things financial makes them an eager audience. However, they are also an ambivalent one as they watch the fallout from the housing bubble and an increasing rate of joblessness, particularly for their generation. Because of this there are concerns that this group of young people may become a Lost Generation, who cannot finance the American Dream at the same level that they might expect. Best practices in marketing from as recently as five years ago may no longer apply when selling to these Millennials.
Too much choice and too little time. Consumers are looking for shortcuts.
Have the Millennials become Generation “Why Bother”?
Can you provide a summary of the challenges facing Millennials when it comes to accessing financial products and services?
items.completion:
The challenges facing Millennials when it comes to accessing financial products and services include:

1. **Complexity and Choice**: The vast array of financial options—such as insurance, banking, and investment services—can be overwhelming, making it difficult for Millennials to navigate and choose the right products.

2. **Lack of Time**: With busy lifestyles, Millennials often lack the time to research and compare financial products, leading to a preference for quick, easy solutions.

3. **Economic Uncertainty**: Concerns about the housing bubble and joblessness create a sense of financial insecurity, making Millennials hesitant to invest or take on debt.

4. **Ambivalence Toward Financial Inclusion**: While Millennials are interested in financial matters, they are also wary of the consequences of financial instability, leading to a cautious approach to financial decisions.

5. **Relevance of Marketing**: Traditional marketing strategies may no longer resonate with Millennials, who prefer more direct, personalized, and socially conscious approaches to financial engagement.

These factors contribute to the potential risk of Millennials becoming a "Lost Generation," as they may struggle to achieve financial stability and the American Dream at the same level as previous generations.

items.prompt:
What is the role of the electro-perception of Mormyrid fishes in their evolution? How might it lead to speciation? Answer according to: BEACON Researchers at Work: Are Electric Fish Magic?
This week’s BEACON Researchers at Work blog post is by postdoc Will Pitchers from the Gallant Lab at MSU.
The fishes in our lab — African freshwater fish called Mormyrids — seem pretty magical when one first hears about them; they spend their lives surrounded by a pulsed electric field they generate themselves, and consequently perceive the world, in some very unintuitive ways, with (proportionately) one of the largest brains of any fish. As they can sense their surroundings electrically they can navigate and hunt in complete darkness, or the murkiest of waters. The output of their electric organs also allows them to communicate with each other over a ‘private channel’ on which their prey (mostly freshwater invertebrates) and predators cannot eavesdrop. None of this is the sense of ‘magical’ that I’m referring to however; I’m talking about ‘magic traits’ as coined by a Prof. Called Sergey Gavrilets (2004) at U. Of Tennessee.
To explain: we know that sometimes speciation can occur in sympatry — i.e. A species splits in two in situ — but it can be hard to find a mechanism for how this happens. What could keep groups separated long enough to evolve apart while they share a habitat? It’s not hard to imagine fishes from one population specializing on one of a couple of food sources, e.g. Some rootling for buried worms while others glean for insect larvae. However, this kind of niche partitioning won’t led to speciation as long as the two groups interbreed. In order to split the species, there also needs to be some reason for the two groups to breed assortatively; preferring mates from their own group rather than the other. A so-called ‘magic trait’ would be one whose evolution could begin to diverge for ecological reasons, but which could also lead to sexual incompatibility. Evolving ‘magic traits’ could therefore put species on the fast-track to speciation.
We think that the electro-perception of Mormyrid fishes might fit this bill quite neatly; since they both hunt and court each other using their electrical pulses. Mormyrids’ electric organ discharges (EOD’s) differ from species to species, and there’s some evidence that these electrical properties influence what features of the fishes’ environment are perceived most clearly (Von der Emde 2006; 2010). It seems likely therefore, that selection could ‘tune’ the EOD to be efficiently detect a preferred food source. On the other hand, we know that Mormyrids identify which fish are the appropriate species with whom to mate by their electrical signals (Feulner et al. 2009) and evidence suggests that females are choosy on the basis of EOD (Schmid & Kramer 2014). The coincidence of the EOD’s importance in both foraging and mate choice might go some way towards explaining why the Mormyrids are so unusually diverse, having split into ~200 species over 20 genera.
Trying to get to the bottom of how the potentially ‘magic’ EOD might be influencing Mormyrid evolution is one of the main research thrusts in the Gallant Lab at present. For my part, I am using the computing resources at iCER to sift through a big heap of next-gen sequencing data to see if we can associate which bits of the genome are associated with variation in the properties of the EOD. As I’m a new recruit to the electric fish scene, I’m going to have to leave you on a cliffhanger, but watch this space for exciting discoveries as we make them!
Emde, Von der, G. 2006. Non-visual environmental imaging and object detection through active electrolocation in weakly electric fish. J. Comp. Physiol. A 192:601–612. Springer-Verlag.
Emde, von der. 2010. 3-Dimensional scene perception during active electrolocation in a weakly electric pulse fish. Front. Behav. Neurosci. 1–13.
Feulner, P. G. D., M. Plath, J. Engelmann, F. Kirschbaum, and R. Tiedemann. 2009. Electrifying love: electric fish use species-specific discharge for mate recognition. Biology Letters 5:225–228. The Royal Society.
Schmid, D., and L. B. Kramer. 2014. Sexual selection by female choice prevents speciation reversal in a hybridizing trio of mormyrid fish in southern Africa: evidence from playback experiments of electric organ discharges. Behaviour 151:1703–1734.
For more information about Will’s work, you can contact him at pitchers at msu dot edu.
items.completion:
The electro-perception of Mormyrid fishes plays a crucial role in their evolution by enabling both ecological specialization and reproductive isolation. This unique ability to generate and interpret electric signals provides a mechanism for **ecological divergence** and **sexual incompatibility**, which are key drivers of speciation.

### 1. **Ecological Specialization and Divergence**
Mormyrids use their electric organ discharges (EODs) to navigate, hunt, and communicate. The variation in EOD properties across species suggests that natural selection can **tune** these signals to optimize for specific ecological niches. For example:
- **Foraging**: EODs may be adjusted to detect preferred prey (e.g., invertebrates) more efficiently in specific environments.
- **Environmental sensing**: The electric field generated by EODs can help Mormyrids perceive their surroundings in three-dimensional space, allowing them to thrive in murky or dark waters.

This **selective pressure** for optimized EODs can lead to **ecological divergence**, where different species evolve distinct EOD patterns to exploit different resources or habitats.

### 2. **Reproductive Isolation via Sexual Incompatibility**
Mormyrids use EODs for **mate recognition**, and females often show **choosy behavior** based on these signals. This **sexual selection** can lead to **assortative mating**, where individuals prefer mates with specific EOD patterns. If two groups evolve distinct EODs, they may **fail to interbreed** (i.e., become **reproductively isolated**), even if they live in the same habitat.

This **reproductive incompatibility** is a classic example of a **"magic trait"**—a trait that promotes ecological divergence but also leads to **sexual incompatibility**, accelerating speciation.

### 3. **Speciation in Sympatry**
The combination of **ecological specialization** and **reproductive isolation** in Mormyrids explains their **high speciation rate**. Despite living in the same area, species can evolve distinct EODs and mating preferences, leading to **speciation in sympatry** (without geographic separation).

### 4. **Research Implications**
Studies like those by Will Pitchers and his team at MSU are critical in understanding how EODs shape evolution. By analyzing genetic variation associated with EOD properties, researchers can trace the evolutionary pathways that led to the diversity observed in Mormyrids.

In summary, the electro-perception of Mormyrids is a **key evolutionary innovation** that drives both ecological specialization and reproductive isolation, making them a prime example of how "magic traits" can lead to rapid speciation.

items.prompt: Write a sonnet about the overwhelming rush of emotions when falling in love with someone unexpected, exploring the themes of surprise, hope, and uncertainty. Use vivid imagery and metaphors to convey the intensity of the experience.
items.completion:
In twilight’s hush, a heart unbound,  
A storm of feelings, wild and wild,  
A love that breaks the quiet vow—  
A spark that lights the darkest night.  

Unexpected, fierce, and true,  
It dances in the soul’s deep chasm,  
A tide that swells with hope and fear,  
A mystery that pulls the heart to calm.  

The world, once still, now echoes with sound—  
A song of joy, a whisper of dread.  
Each glance, a verse, each touch, a bond,  
A fleeting flame that burns in the dark.  

Yet shadows loom, unsure and vast,  
A future stitched with threads of doubt.  
But in the heart, a spark still glows—  
A love that dares to chase the unknown.

items.prompt: Please write an essay of at least 500 words that tells the detailed story of a time when you reunited with someone you had not seen in a long time. Include in your story your feelings leading up to the reunion, the emotions you experienced during the actual reunion, your thoughts and conversations during your time together, and any lessons or insights you gained from the experience. Feel free to use descriptive language and creative storytelling techniques to bring the story to life.
items.completion:
**A Reunion That Changed Everything**

The day I finally reunited with my long-lost cousin, Maria, was a mix of emotions that I had been preparing for, yet I never expected to feel so deeply. It was a rainy afternoon in the spring of 2023, and I had been searching for her for over a decade. The memories of our last meeting were hazy, but the anticipation of seeing her again was electrifying.

I had last seen Maria when I was 16, after a series of misadventures that left us both feeling lost and disconnected. She had moved to a different city, and I had drifted into a life that didn’t align with my past. The silence between us had grown thick, and the distance had become a barrier that I couldn’t ignore. I had tried to reach out, but the words never felt right, and the time never felt enough. It was only when I stumbled upon an old photo of us in a local park that I knew I had to find her.

The journey to her home was long and filled with anticipation. As I arrived, the rain had stopped, and the air was crisp, carrying the scent of earth and fresh rain. I stepped into her porch, and the moment I saw her, I felt a wave of emotions—hope, nostalgia, and a flicker of joy. She was older than I remembered, but her smile was the same, and her eyes, filled with the same warmth I had lost, made me feel like I had been waiting for her all along.

We greeted each other with a hug that felt like a lifeline, and the silence that followed was broken only by the sound of our hearts beating in sync. As we talked, the memories of our past flooded back. We shared stories of our childhood, the moments we had missed, and the reasons we had drifted apart. It was a conversation that felt like a journey through time, and I realized how much I had changed, yet how much of me had stayed the same.

During our time together, we laughed, reminisced, and even shared a few moments of quiet reflection. I learned that Maria had moved to a new city for a better life, but she still missed the warmth of her old home. She told me about her new job, her friends, and the challenges of adapting to a new culture. In return, I shared my struggles with the past, the loneliness I had felt, and the hope I had for the future.

The reunion taught me a valuable lesson: that connection is not just about the past, but about the present and the future. It reminded me that even though time can separate us, it doesn’t have to define us. We are all part of a larger story, and even in the silence between us, there is always a chance to reconnect.

As we said our goodbyes, I felt a profound sense of peace. The reunion was more than just seeing someone I had lost track of—it was a reminder of the strength of human connection, the power of forgiveness, and the beauty of being open to the people who matter most. It was a moment that would stay with me forever, a testament to the idea that even in the darkest times, there is always light, and that light can come from the most unexpected places.

items.prompt: Provide a descriptive narration of the creative process of a surrealist artist as they paint a surreal landscape full of elements that defy gravity and reality.
items.completion:
The creative process of a surrealist artist begins with a spark of inspiration, often born from the subconscious mind. As they sit before their canvas, the air is thick with possibility, and the world around them dissolves into a realm of dreams. The artist's hand moves with a fluidity that transcends the constraints of reality, guided by a deep well of imagination.

In this moment, the landscape unfolds like a dream: a sky that swirls with colors that defy the laws of physics, where clouds float in impossible formations, and the sun casts shadows that dance in a way that defies logic. Trees grow in the middle of the ocean, their roots reaching into the sky, while buildings spiral upwards, their structures twisting in a manner that suggests a forgotten civilization.

The artist's brush strokes are not merely tools of expression but extensions of their thoughts, each movement a whisper of the surreal. They might paint a river that flows backward, its waters reflecting not the world around it, but a dream of a place never seen. A bridge connects an island to a floating city, suspended in the air, its towers reaching toward the clouds as if seeking something beyond the reach of gravity.

As the painting takes shape, the artist's mind becomes a conduit for the impossible. They might introduce elements that challenge the viewer's perception—figures that walk on water, animals that transform into objects, or landscapes that shift and morph with the passing of time. Each element is a fragment of the artist's inner world, a fragment of the dream that they seek to capture.

The process is not linear; it is a dance of intuition and exploration. The artist may pause, allowing the canvas to breathe, before making a final stroke that seals the vision. In this act, they are not merely painting; they are weaving a narrative that exists beyond the confines of reality, inviting the viewer to wander into a world where the impossible is not just possible, but inevitable.

Here is a piece of text: Italy have named two Rio 2016 Paralympic gold medallists in their squad for this year's World Para Swimming Championships in Mexico City.
Both Federico Morlacchi and Francesco Bocciardo have been named in a 13-strong team for the event, which will be held between September 30 and October 6.
Morlacchi, already a double World Championship gold medallist, won the SM9 200 metres medley title in Brazil, as well as three silver medals.
Bocciardo's gold came in the S6 400m freestyle division.
Other members of Italy's team include double Rio 2016 silver medallist Francesco Bettella and Giulia Ghiretti, who won silver and bronze in the Brazilian city.
Nineteen-year-old Monica Boggioni is also one to watch after she broke S4 100m and 200m world records this season.
The other members of the squad are Simone Barlaam, Vincenzo Boni, Cecilia Camellini, Antonio Fantin, Carlotta Gill, Efrem Morelli, Alessia Scortechini and Arjola Trimi.
Sixty countries are set to be represented in Mexico City in all, with 550 swimmers due to compete at the Francisco Marquez Olympic Swimming Pool.
Competition will take place between September 30 and October 6 with the competition the first major event of the Tokyo 2020 cycle.
The Powerlifting World Championships will also take place in the Mexican capital at the same time as part of a festival of Para-sport.

Based on the text material above, generate the response to the following quesion or instruction: What other Para-sport event will take place in the Mexican capital at the same time as the Powerlifting World Championships?
items.completion: The text states that the Powerlifting World Championships will also take place in Mexico City at the same time as the Para-sport festival. Therefore, the other Para-sport event that will take place in Mexico City at the same time as the Powerlifting World Championships is the **Para-sport festival**.

items.prompt: Design a chart that presents similarities and differences of at least five different social media platforms, including Instagram, TikTok, Facebook, Snapchat, and Twitter, with regards to their purpose, demographics, features, privacy settings, algorithm, content format, and user engagement.
items.completion:
**Social Media Platform Comparison Chart: Instagram, TikTok, Facebook, Snapchat, and Twitter**

| **Criteria**               | **Instagram**                          | **TikTok**                            | **Facebook**                          | **Snapchat**                        | **Twitter**                         |
|---------------------------|----------------------------------------|---------------------------------------|----------------------------------------|--------------------------------------|------------------------------------|
| **Purpose**                | Visual storytelling, photo sharing     | Short-form video content               | Community building, social networking | Snappy, ephemeral content            | Real-time communication, news, opinions |
| **Demographics**           | Younger users (18–34), females dominate | Younger users (13–24), males dominate | Broad age range, diverse demographics  | Teenagers and young adults           | Adults, professionals, influencers |
| **Features**               | Photos, Stories, Reels, Highlights     | Short videos, Challenges, duets       | Posts, comments, groups, events        | Snaps, lenses, filters, direct messages | Posts, tweets, retweets, replies      |
| **Privacy Settings**       | Moderate control (user can manage)     | Moderate control (user can manage)    | High control (user can manage)         | Low control (ephemeral content)      | Moderate control (user can manage)  |
| **Algorithm**              | Focus on engagement, likes, shares     | Focus on watch time, views, shares    | Focus on engagement, likes, shares     | Focus on views, shares, engagement   | Focus on engagement, likes, shares  |
| **Content Format**         | Photos, videos, stories, reels         | Short videos, challenges, duets       | Text, images, videos, links            | Snaps, lenses, filters, direct messages | Text, images, videos, threads        |
| **User Engagement**        | High (likes, comments, shares)          | High (views, shares, challenges)       | Moderate (likes, comments, shares)      | Low (ephemeral, limited engagement)  | Moderate (likes, retweets, replies)  |

### **Summary of Similarities and Differences**

**Similarities:**
- All platforms allow users to share content and interact with others.
- All have features for creating and sharing content (e.g., photos, videos, text).
- All use algorithms to curate content based on user preferences and behavior.
- All cater to a broad age range, though with varying demographics.

**Differences:**
- **Purpose:** Instagram focuses on visual storytelling, TikTok on short-form video, Facebook on community building, Snapchat on ephemeral content, and Twitter on real-time communication.
- **Demographics:** Instagram and Facebook have broader age ranges, while TikTok and Snapchat target younger audiences.
- **Privacy:** Snapchat has the lowest privacy control, while Facebook has the highest.
- **Algorithm:** TikTok prioritizes watch time, while Instagram focuses on engagement metrics.
- **Content Format:** Instagram and Facebook support text and images, while TikTok and Snapchat focus on short videos.

This chart provides a clear comparison to help users understand the unique strengths and use cases of each platform.

items.prompt: Write a Perl program that accepts user input for an array of integers, applies the merge sort algorithm to sort the array in descending order, and prints the sorted integers to the console.
